intro: Lycoctonum pteropus, also known as Tian Keng, is a plant species in the family Ranunculaceae, characterized by its unique leaf and flower morphology. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Lycoctonum pteropus include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, particularly lyconitin and tiankengine, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Lycoctonum pteropus is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, or made into a capsule or tablet, to take advantage of its therapeutic benefits.
